title: BroadcastBox session record extension
description: Fields the broadcast-box module contributes to `session` records via its manifest `capabilities.schemaExtensions: ["session"]` (loaded by ModuleResolver from this path). Capture/schedule provenance for meetings captured by a BroadcastBox appliance, plus the AI transcript trust label. NOTE: `visibility` and `minutes_status` are CORE session fields (record-type-schemas/session-schema.json), not here; the A/V blob itself is an `attached_files[]` entry on the record (UUID storage), referenced by `capture.av_file`.

capture.description: Provenance of an appliance-captured recording. Mirrors the device→CP `session.manifest` capture block (@civicpress/broadcast-protocol).

device.description: Enrolled BroadcastBox device id that produced the recording.

av_file.description: Storage UUID of the recorded A/V, stored as an attached_files[] entry. Its presence (with no transcript_status) is what marks a session as needing transcription.

segments.description: Time segments enabling per-segment visibility (e.g. an in-camera portion within an otherwise public meeting). The transcription service MUST exclude in_camera segments.

visibility.description: Whether this segment is published. in_camera segments are excluded from publication, livestream, and transcription.

schedule.description: Pre-meeting scheduling pushed to the assigned device so it can capture autonomously even without connectivity.

assigned_device.description: Enrolled device id assigned to capture this session.

transcript_status.description: Verification state of the AI transcript. 'automated' = unverified (display labeled, A/V is authoritative); 'reviewed' = a clerk has checked it. ABSENT (with capture.av_file present) = needs transcription. NOTE: the worker claim/fail states are a pending W2 sub-decision (see docs/specs/2026-06-20-transcription-service-design.md §10.3).
